summary: No ship: the root-cause seam is sound, but HEAD no longer proves a single locked RED→GREEN flip, and ref ownership remains detached from auto-merge authorization.

findings.title: Blocker: the locked regression partition changes between RED and HEAD
findings.body: The committed RED record ran 15 regression cases, while the unchanged tag-filter command selects 37 at HEAD. W8/W9 and W20-W24 were added after red.sha alongside new disarm and ownership behavior, so those cases were never RED at the locked baseline. A future GREEN record would therefore compare different test sets and cannot prove a same-test, single-behavior flip.
findings.recommendation: Keep the original locked witnesses as this bugfix's stable regression partition. Move the later observable behavior changes into separately RED-pinned bugfixes, or reroute the composite change through the feature pipeline.

findings.title: Blocker: ownership proof does not protect auto-merge authorization
findings.body: `assertOurCommit` runs only for adopt/rebuild. A writer-authored PR whose head was replaced by another actor remains trusted on CLEAN/BLOCKED/UNKNOWN skip; the bump lane can preserve or add auto-merge without proving that head. Conversely, an armed DIRTY propose-pr with a foreign head fails ownership validation before reaching `--disable-auto`, leaving stale authorization active. This is the unclosed skip/authorization half of the r5 ownership risk.
findings.recommendation: Treat provenance as an input to authorization reconciliation, not only force-push permission. Never retain or add auto-merge for an unproven head, and revoke stale authorization before any ownership check that may abort. Add foreign-head skip and armed propose-pr failure witnesses.

findings.title: Test-quality: the ownership call-site witness checks dead text, not effective commit metadata
findings.body: The new witness only greps for expected `git config` and `git commit` literals, then checks that `BUMP_COMMIT_MESSAGE` appears somewhere in the executor. A later config override, environment override, or amend can change the actual remote commit while every assertion remains green, causing adopt/rebuild to fail closed indefinitely.
findings.recommendation: Extend the hermetic workflow-step witness to capture the effective author, committer, and final HEAD message passed into the executor ownership path, or centralize commit construction and ownership expectations in one shared typed contract.

next_steps: Restore a stable single-flip RED/ GREEN partition.
Replace the static ownership witness with an effective-behavior witness.
